name: env-continuity-dedup
description: Measures environmental continuity fact dedup ratio across repeated walking turns. Starts player on a named street, walks east 8 times, then reads the continuity block character count from the last turn. The dedup pass in assembleContinuityPacket should keep continuity_block_chars under 1000. This is a distributional probe to confirm the dedup is working across random seeds, not just one fixed seed.
prompt_cycle: I am standing on Main Street in a quiet town, in front of a row of weathered buildings with cracked pavement and a red-and-white awning. A rusted mailbox stands crooked nearby.
I am walking along a cobblestone lane in an old village. Stone cottages with thatched roofs line both sides. A wooden sign creaks above a pub door.
I stand at the edge of a dusty desert road. Adobe buildings with sun-bleached walls stretch ahead. A worn cactus fence borders the path.
I am on the wooden boardwalk of a coastal town. Salt-worn shop fronts with faded signs face the harbor. A bell buoy clangs in the distance.
I am in a narrow alley between tall brick buildings. Fire escapes zigzag up the walls. A single streetlamp flickers overhead.
